race.name: !!! Event Canceled !!!
race.description:
Special Operations Warrior Foundation: Donate to SOWF

              “Our mission is driven by a desire to honor the sacrifice made in service to our country by providing “cradle to career” educational support to children of fallen Special Operations Personnel and spouses as well as all Medal of Honor Recipients. From a “battlefield promise” made in 1980, the SOWF program has grown to include immediate financial support to severely wounded, injured, and ill Special Operators. By providing this support, SOWF aims to help these families build brighter futures despite the challenges they face due to the loss of a loved one.”

Event Canceled! Thank you for your support, however, we are unable to set-up this event without community support and I am unable to cover the cost this year. 

The Event:

              What: 24-HR Endurance run, walk, jog! This 4 mile loop, featuring a 200 ft. elevation gain at Boat Hill and ~300 ft. for one completed loop. Challenge your will and ability! Will you be the first person to finish 100 miles in 24hrs? While this race will be held for the full 24 hrs., any distance from all participants will be recorded to show their input and dedication to supporting SOWF for the total miles completed by all participants at the end of the event. Need an incentive to compete, the one who runs the longest distance will receive the Start/Finish Banner and a plaque recognizing them as The Airstrip Apex Predator as trophies. Last year, first place ran away with banner finishing 60 miles.

             When: 5th - 7th June, run starts @ 8 am on the 6th and will end @ 8 am the 7th. Overnight camping can start at 4pm on the 5th. Payment for camping will be taken care of at the airstrip. Camping costs $12 per family per night, paid in cash or check at the event. This will be a pack-in, pack-out event; there will be extra garbage cans to take care of extra trash. Open fires will follow the fire bans when the event occurs. (Collection of firewood is prohibited on state park grounds and military reservation.)

               Who: Anyone! It's a free event! Show up and put in your support for those who gave it all! 

               What else: There will not be food or water readily available for purchase. The aid stations on the route will have FREE water and electrolytes for use. There will also be tools to stretch out and recover during and after the race. 

                Aid stations:  will be located at the base of 200 ft incline of Boat Hill and the SW loop. The starting line will house the camping, staging, flag ceremonies, Porta-potties, and any other vendors we may be able to have.

                Recommended Runner Gear: Headlamp if running through the night. Personal Camping gear if staying either night. Food, water, recovery essentials, mid-run electrolytes, snacks, water-packs, etc. 

Timeline:

June 5th - 4pm - 10pm | Arrivals for those wishing to camp the night before the race are welcome to show up and pay then.

June 6th - 6 am | Arrivals of volunteers and runners, welcome!

6:30 - 7:30 am | Runners present will be given their bibs, day of sign-ups are welcome!  Those arriving on the day of the race and wishing to start at 8 am need to be present at this time.

7:45 am | Troop 400 will raise the flags.

8:00 am | RACE BEGINS!!! (Day Route). Payment for camping and donations can be made any point after 8 am. 

Sunset / 9:30pm | Race will move to Night route

June 7th – 

Sunrise / 5:30am | Move back to the 4-mile loop

8:00 am | Race ends!

8:30 am | Ceremony in memory of those SOWF represents and crowning the furthest distance ran with Start/Finish Banner and announcing the total miles covered by all participants!

All payments for camping need to be made by 10 am on the 7th.

events.name: SOWF Ultra
events.details:
Any distance made during the event will be tracked for a TOTAL overall distance ran, walked, jogged, to support the heroes in our lives by all participants. 
HOWEVER, run the furthest and go home with the start finish banner!

race.name: "5K Run & Walk for a Great Cause" organized by Indian American Chamber of Commerce Greater Cincinnati & Northern Kentucky
race.description:
Lace up, show up, make a difference-one step at a time!

 

When & Where

Date: August 9th, 2026, 9:00 am (Registrations starts at 8:00 am)

Location: Corwin M Nixon Park

6249 Mason Montgomery Rd, Mason OH-45040

 

Why We Run & Walk

This isn't just a 5k; it's a chance to move together for something bigger than us. Every stride, every mile, every smile helps supports organizations like Ekal Vidyalaya, Adopt A Class and IACC that are making a difference in our communities.

Ekal Vidyalaya Foundation

Ekal Vidyalaya works to bring education to children in rural and tribal areas of India through a unique one-teacher school model. These schools provide basic education, digital literacy, and life skills to children in remote villages where access to education is limited. By supporting Ekal, we help empower communities through education and opportunity.

Adopt A Class Foundation

Adopt A Class parters with local schools to support teachers and students by providing funding for classroom resources, educational programs, mentorship opportunities. Their work helps strengthen local classrooms and ensures students have the tools they need to succeed.

Every registration and donation helps support these important initiatives and creates lasting impact both locally and globally.

Whether you're running for a personal best or walking to enjoy the day, your steps make an impact.

 

For Sponsorship details, please contact:

Nidhi Bedi: 513-378-5453

Sapna Susarla: 513-289-2975

Lakshya Arora 614-218-9141

race.name: "Be Your Own Hero" - 6th Annual Trevor Kononenko Memorial Event
race.description:
Join us at the 6th Annual 'Be Your Own Hero' Golf Tournament honoring the life and memory of Trevor Kononenko. 

Taking place on Saturday, September 19, 2026 at Stow Acres Country Club, Stow, MA in support of Active Minds. Singles, doubles, and foursomes golf options are available for registration, as well as, a raffle and auction after the golf event concludes. 

Active Minds supports youth and young adults nationwide with the resources and trainings to take action around mental health in their community. Donations made on this webpage are tax-deductible donations to Active Minds. Together, we are championing a new era of mental health!

events.name: 1 Round of Golf
events.details:
Thank you for registering for the 'Be Your Own Hero' Trevor K Memorial Golf Tournament. This registration option includes: one round of golf at Stow Acres Country Club.

We remember Trevor through hosting an Annual Memorial Golf Tournament with all of Trevor’s family, friends, and friends of friends that helps us keep his spirit alive.

A $25 donation is included in the price of this registration. Active Minds is the nation's leading non-profit mobilizing youth and young adults to forever change how mental health is valued and prioritized in society. 

If you are interest in a pair or foursome, simply click "add participant" and provide those individual's information.

race.name: "Beer Me 5k" presented by Tucker Brewing Company
race.description:
🍺 BEER ME 5K IS BACK — AND BIGGER THAN EVER
Get ready for the 3rd Annual Beer Me 5K, presented by Tucker Brewing Company—where a fast, flat race meets an unforgettable after party.

🏃‍♂️ THE RUN
Start and finish right at the brewery before heading out on a super flat course through the surrounding industrial and residential streets. Whether you’re chasing a PR or just here for a good time, this course is built for everyone.

🍻 THE FINISH LINE
Cross the line and step straight into the beer garden—because your reward is waiting: an ice-cold Tucker Lager in hand the moment you finish.  Award ceremony will follow shortly after the race.

🌯 FUEL UP
Want to level up your morning? Pre-purchase a breakfast burrito and have it ready post-race. You’ve earned it.

🔥 THE AFTER PARTY
This is where things really take off.  Live music. Cold plunges. Yard Games. Local vendors. High-energy vibes all around.

It’s not just a race—it’s a full-on experience that keeps going long after you cross the finish line.

🎉 WHY YOU DON’T WANT TO MISS THIS
Because it’s not every day you get to run a 5K and walk straight into #4 Best Beer Garden in the America (voted & awarded by USA Today)

Run it. Walk it. Celebrate it.

👉 SIGN UP. SHOW UP. BEER UP.
 
For more info about how awesome Tucker Brewing is CLICK HERE

race.name: "Bridge to Brew"  Half Marathon and 5K
race.description:
Join us for the 4th Bridge to Brew Half Marathon and 5K in downtown Port Huron.  Be part of the "Quay street experience" near the Start/Finish area.  

Our course will run along the blue waters of the St Clair river and Lake Huron. Both races are an out and back. We will have water stations every 2 miles along half marathon.  We will also have live music at start/finish area along the banks of the Black River. All  finishers will also receive a finisher medal. Age group awards will be given out to top 3 finishers in each division. 1st place in each age group will receive a super cool ON prize package. Overall male and female winners will receive a free pair of ON shoes at Elite Feet of Port Huron. Parking will be located throughout downtown near start/ finish area.

Packet pick up will be Sat August 8th from 10-3 at Elite Feet of Port Huron 327 Huron Ave. Day of race packet pick up will begin at 6:00 am at start/finish line. ON shoe reps will also have demo shoes to try out at packet pick up and race morning.  Put a pair on and go for a run in them. Also starting at 6:00 am race morning Sunday August 9th near start/finish area on Quay St in front of Wings Ect.  During and following all race's, Bootleggers will have a great breakfast buffet and serving ice cold beverages inside and outside. Our ON reps will also be setup with lots of demo shoes to race in.

When signing up please note there are Men's and Woman's options for shirt.

Eastside timing will provide timing services

race.name: "Copper the Bloodhound's K9 Mile RUCK/Walk" Benefitting Chesapeake K9 Fund
race.description:
Join us for a fun challenge at the "Copper the Bloodhound's K9 Mile Ruck/Walk" on Saturday, June 6, 2026, in Annapolis, MD! 

This event, benefitting the Chesapeake K9 Fund, is a unique way to explore Downtown Historic Annapolis with a weighted ruck or just by walking. 

At 9am Saturday, you will be given the waypoints you must find.   They will not be in order!  Being with a group is most helpful to find the optimal route, but not required. Plan your route, hit the waypoints, and enjoy the sights over a minimum of 9 miles. 

Event Details:

Date: June 6, 2026
Location: Stan & Joe's Saloon 37 West Street, Annapolis, MD   21401
Activities: Rucking or Walking


Entry Fee: $30 per person-Rucking with weight or walking without weight.  People rucking will be required to carry 20lbs but under 175lbs will carry 10lbs. You supply your own weight.  Those people Rucking will be eligible to win the coveted belt and bragging rights!

Start & Finish and the After Party: Stan & Joe's Annapolis.

Recommended Gear:

Cell phone to check in your weighpoints.

Hydration

A positive attitude!


All proceeds go to the Chesapeake K9 Fund to support first responder K9 units. Participants receive a Ruck Patch, and there's plenty of fun to be had with giveaways, raffles, and silent auctions.   This is a dog friendly event.

race.name: "I Can" Triathlon
race.description:
WHO: Children ages 4-16 with different abilities; child does NOT have to live in Miami County to participate

WHAT: An adapted triathlon (as much assistance as needed)

WHEN: Sunday, July 26, 2026 from 9:00 to 12:00 (line up begins at 8:45)

WHERE: Run/Walk/Walker/Gait Trainer/Roll – Hobart Arena Parking Lot #1
                Bike/Tryke - Hobart Arena Parking Lot #2
                Swim - Troy Aquatic Park


Proceeds benefit Miamibucs, a non-profit organization that provides Amtrykes for  children and adults with disabilities who live in Miami County
$25 per child - includes a T-shirt* , medal, goodie bag, and family pool party after triathlon

★ Participation with assistance is possible for ALL skill levels! Race course is adapted to each participant’s abilities.

★ In need of a tryke? Contact us at least 2 weeks before the event! We have a limited number of sizes of trial trykes available for use.

★ One parent/relative/family friend is required to be with each participant throughout the triathlon. A buddy will be assigned on race day to provide guidance and assist with equipment from the beginning of the race to the finish line. Special volunteers assist all children in the pool (parents do not have to get into the pool).

★ Limited to first 75 participants; financial assistance available if needed.

Contact Faye McNerney at 937-335-4013 or miamibucs@gmail.com for further information. Additional information will be provided by email to race participants.

race.name: "Live Your Dash" The Dee Smith Memorial 5K Fun walk/run
race.description:
Dee Smith, a lifetime Ogden resident and a Weber County leader, was a proponent of keeping our community safe. As a former county prosecutor, leader, mentor, and teacher, Dee was a diligent civil leader to local law enforcement officers.

Dee passed away unexpectedly in June 2021 and left behind a legacy of hard work along with his amazing family and countless friends. Dee was passionate about being active and loved biking, running, hiking, and any outdoor activity under the "Blue Dome." He lived life to the fullest, by living "his dash," and is greatly missed.

The purpose of the race is to honor Dee's memory and a make a thoughtful donation to support local programs important to Dee. over the last 3 years over $80,000 in race proceeds have gone to various Local law enforcement agencies, Weber State University for Law Enforcement and Criminal Justice scholarship programs. We couldn't have done it without you and this year we are hoping for it to be BIGGER and BETTER with your continued support!


Your race fee(s), donations, are tax deductible.  We will email you a donation receipt.  Please keep your receipt as it is your official record to claim your donation as a tax deduction. 

Thank you for being a part of Dee's legacy!  For more information, please contact us at : liveyourdashdeesmith@gmail.com.

Race Fees

  • Pre-Race Fee (must register by 05/04/2025 to guarantee T-Shirt)
    • Adults (18 and older) $25.00.  Registration includes a commemorative shirt.
    • Youth (5 to 17 year old) $10.00.  Registration includes a commemorative shirt.
    • Children (4 and under) Free- No shirt Included
  • Late Registration ( after 5/4/2025)
    • Adults (18 and older) $25.00. T-shirts are not guaranteed; subject to availability
      Youth (17 and under) $10.00. T-shirts are not guaranteed; subject to availability
      Children (4 and under) Free
  • Day of Race Fee:
    • Adults (18 and older) $30.00. T-shirts are not guaranteed; subject to availability
    • Youth (17 and under) $15.00. T-shirts are not guaranteed; subject to availability
    • Children (4 and under) Free
  • First place winners (male, female, male and female youth) will win a commemorative keepsake

race.name: "LOOP THE LAKE" The Great New York State Marathon Weekend
race.description:
LOOP THE LAKE!!   A celebration of the Great State of New York, Races passes through a beautiful tree lined course that loops around Onondaga Lake.  The weekend kicks off the morning of Saturday October 18th with the 5k and 10k runs. Then continues the morning of Sunday October 19th with the full and half marathon's. 

EXCELSIOR CHALLENGE!  Test your endurance and achieve New York State acclaim! When you complete both a Saturday and Sunday race (5k/10k and half/full marathon) you'll earn the prestigious Great New York EXCELSIOR Challenge Medal - in addition to your 5k/10k finisher and half/full marathon medals.

Sign up for one of our Excelsior Challenges and hit the trifecta of medals!

race.name: "Love, Jill" Memorial 5k and 1 Mile Walk
race.description:
Join us for the 1st Annual “Love, Jill” Memorial Race, a 5K charity run and 1-mile walk through the streets of Beaver along the beautiful Ohio River on August 15, 2026. This event is held in loving memory of Jill Renee Littler, who passed away on September 22, 2025, after a courageous battle with oral cavity squamous cell carcinoma.

Jill’s strength was forged long before her cancer diagnosis. At just 15 years old, she was diagnosed with Crohn’s Disease, a fight she carried with her for 36 years. Through countless surgeries, hospital stays, treatments, and daily challenges, Jill faced her illness with quiet resilience, grit, and grace—never allowing it to define her or diminish the love she gave to others.

In early 2025, Jill was diagnosed with oral cavity squamous cell carcinoma. She approached this battle with the same determination and grace that defined her life. On May 2nd, Jill underwent major surgery that included extensive lymph node removal, partial mandible (jaw) removal, and complex reconstruction using a titanium plate, followed by reconstructive surgery. Her treatment continued with 33 rounds of radiation and multiple rounds of chemotherapy. Despite setbacks, severe side effects, and overwhelming physical challenges, Jill pushed forward with unwavering resolve, always focused on her family and the people she loved.

In August 2025, Jill was hospitalized after developing a blood clot in her lung. Following additional procedures, doctors discovered the cancer had spread. With love, clarity, and extraordinary courage, her family made the difficult decision to bring her home, where she passed peacefully surrounded by her loved ones on September 22, 2025. Race weekend falls on Jill’s birthday, making this gathering an especially meaningful celebration of her life.

Jill was the heart of her family and a bright light in the Beaver County community. She lived guided by the values she cherished—love of family, service to others, a strong work ethic, kindness, and gentle leadership. Whether as a coworker, friend, volunteer, or quiet supporter behind the scenes, Jill had a rare way of lifting people up. This event honors not only her memory, but the compassion, strength, and grace she shared so freely with everyone she met.

Participants may choose between a 5K run or a 1-mile fun walk. The event will also feature a Chinese auction and a 50/50 raffle.

All registered participants will receive a commemorative race t-shirt, available for pickup at the event.

 
Awards
Plaques awarded to the Top Overall Male and Female finishers
Medals awarded to the Top 3 Male and Female finishers in each age group:

15 & Under
16–19
20–29
30–39
40–49
50 & Over
 
Why “Love, Jill”?

Jill ended every note, message, and reminder the same way she lived her life—with love. To honor that legacy, her family created the annual “Love, Jill” Scholarship, transforming her signature into a lasting gift for young people in our community. Through this scholarship, Jill’s message continues to inspire future generations:

“I believe in you.”

We invite the entire Beaver County community to join us in celebrating Jill’s life, honoring her legacy, and showing her family just how deeply she was loved.

race.name: "Maranacook Lake Association and Watershed Friends on the 4th" 5K Run/Walk
race.description:
2026 "Maranacook Lake Association and Watershed Friends on the 4th" 5K Run and/or Walk

We are excited about our ongoing partnership between Maranacook Lake Association and the Watershed Friends to continue this beloved community event on its 24th anniversary!! Start the 4th of July off on the right foot and join the gang at Central Maine's most popular race!

The "Friends on the 4th" 5K course is a forgiving, "feel good" race open for all runners, joggers and walkers. After a loop through downtown Main Street, most of the course follows the beautiful eastern shore of Maranacook Lake.  

 

Check in is at 7:00 am. Same day registration 7:00 - 7:45 am. The race starts at 8:00 am.

 

Awards:
Men & Women, 1st place awards for the following age groups: 10 and under male and female, 11-14 male and female; 15-18 male and female; 19-29 male and female; 30-39 male and female; 40-49 male and female; 50-59 male and female; 60-69 male and female; 70 and over male and female.

 

Payment and registration form must be received by midnight, EST on Thursday, June 18, 2026, in order to guarantee a race T-shirt. 

Online registration is open until 1:00 pm EST on July 3, 2026. 

 

Last minute registration on race day may begin at 7:00 am* at the American Legion, 117 Bowdoin Street, and ENDS at 7:45 am. To expedite the same day registration process we strongly encourage you to bring cash. A $40 registration fee will be charged. In order to encourage advance registration and to maximize the fundraising dollars that go back to preserving our watershed, t-shirts will not be available for same day registrants or online registrants after June 18th due to ordering deadlines. 

 

Questions?  Contact Race Co-Chairs, Cami Wilbert or Peter Washburn via email Friendsonthe4th5K@gmail.com.

 

For the safety of runners, parking is not allowed along the race route.  Parking attendants will guide participants & spectators to designated parking areas.  

 

*Same day registrations may be limited or not available depending on state of Maine regulations. PRE-REGISTRATION IS HIGHLY RECOMMENDED. Due to ordering deadlines, register by June 18th to be guaranteed a T-shirt.

events.name: "Maranacook Lake Association and Watershed Friends on the 4th" 5K - TIMED

race.name: "Miles That Matter" 5K & 10K  Run/Walk
race.description:
This year we want to celebrate America's 250th Birthday!  Sign up for the STARS & STRIPES 5k or 10k and receive a special Miles That Matter Medal when you cross the finish line.

 Get ready for an incredible morning of movement, purpose, and community! Join us on July 11, 2025 for the ACTS Miles That Matter Run/Walk 5K & 10K in beautiful Warsaw, Indiana. This annual event brings runners and walkers together for an inspiring experience on one of the most scenic courses in the area. Participants will enjoy stunning views of Pike Lake and finish strong as the course winds through the beautiful Beyer Farm Trail. Many runners say it’s a course they look forward to every year, and once you experience it, you’ll see why! Water stations will be available along the route to keep you refreshed and energized along the way.

Both the 5K and 10K races are professionally timed, so you can expect quick and accurate results shortly after you cross the finish line. The 5K includes 5-year age group divisions, with awesome awards for the top three finishers in each age group. The 10K features 9-year age group divisions, also awards to the top three in each category. In addition, we will celebrate the overall male and female winners in both race distances. 

After the race, relax and celebrate your achievement with refreshments waiting at the finish, including fruit and cookies. Our event is proudly timed by TH Timing, known as one of the best race timing teams in the area, ensuring a smooth and professional race-day experience.

Most importantly, every step you take makes a difference. By participating in the Miles That Matter Run/Walk. Lace up your shoes, bring your friends and family, and come run or walk with purpose—because every mile truly matters! 🏃‍♂️✨

  There is an entry fee for online registration and taxes.  

Pre-registration is open now until July 5th.  Includes t-shirt and fee is  $25 plus fees.  After July 5th  registration is $30 plus fees and no shirt is guaranteed.

5K age group: 14 & under; 15-19; 20-24; 25-29; 30-34; 35-39; 40-44; 45-49; 50-54; 55-59; 60-64; 65 & over

10K age group: 16 & under; 17-25; 26-34; 35-43; 44-52; 53 & over.

race.name: "Paso a Paso's 5k Run/Walk to End Diabetes"
race.description:
“Paso a Paso’s Inagural 5k Run/Walk to End Diabetes”
Join us for our First Annual community event supporting diabetes care and awareness
June 6, 2026
Mission Hills Park
3800 O'Keefe
7:30 am Start time

REGISTRATION FEES
Individual registration:  
$35 Early Bird (April 2 to April 16)
$40 (April 17 to May 22)
$45 (May 23rd to June 5th)

Team registration: $5 discount per team member 
The first 9 team members will pay the regular price and receive a $5 refund once the 10th team member signs up. Every new team member who joins after the 10th will pay the discounted fee.
(TEAMS MUST HAVE A MINIMUM OF 10 MEMBERS TO QUALIFY FOR TEAM DISCOUNT)
Race Day Registration:  $50

PRE-REGISTERED (BY May 8th ) RUNNERS & WALKERS RECEIVE RACE T-SHIRT

REGISTRAION FEES ARE NON-REFUNDABLE  

***EARLY PACKET PICK UP on May 31 @ 700 North Copia from 9 am-5 pm

RACE DAY PACKET PICK UP: @ the start line 6:00 am

AWARDS & PRIZES!!!
Refreshments available at the finish line

Trophy to Overall Male & Female
Trophy to LARGEST TEAM
Medals to Top 3 finishers in male & female age groups
0-14, 15-19, 20-29, 30-39, 40-49, 50-59, 60-69, 70 & Over

**COURSE DESCRIPTION:  This 5K course takes you through the beautiful neighborhood around Mission Hills Park.

PROCEEDS BENEFIT:
All proceeds help Paso a Paso Diabetes Initiative Non-Profit deliver life-changing diabetes care, education, and support to underserved families in our community.

events.name: 5k
events.details: ***EARLY PACKET PICK UP on April 31 @ 700 North Copia from 9am-5pm

race.name: "Run For A Vet"
race.description:
Sponsored by Branson Veterans of America 913 (BVA913) to honor our Veterans. This race helps to raise money for veterans that are in need.   

The Branson Veterans of America 913 is a not-for-profit corporation dedicated to support all American veterans, focusing on helping veterans and their families from all areas of service.  We do this by:

1.      Promoting honor to all who have patriotically served our country in any of the Armed Forces of the United States of America through organization and support of public gatherings and ceremonies.

2.      Supporting education of patriotism, duties of citizenship, and honor of our Flag through the organization’s scholarship program, support and encouragement of local educational institutions for patriotic education, and individual conduct within the communities.

3.      Assisting fellow veterans and their families through physical and economic response to special needs, encouragement and support for a healthy lifestyle, and a sense of community.

4.      Working in partnership with local government and private agencies to assure an environment for safe and secure living for all veterans and their families.

                                                      BRANSON VETERANS OF AMERICA 913 VISION STATEMENT:

The Branson Veterans of America 913 vision is to be a prominent organization for ensuring veterans of all eras are respected for their service and for promoting honor for the United States of America.

Location: Dewey Short Visitors Center, 4500 State Hwy 165 Branson, MO

Packet Pickup Friday 5-7pm. Race day registration from 6:00 to 6:30am. (Located at the beginning of the running trail)

Half Marathon will begin at 6:45am – 5K will begin at 7:30am

Race Course: Dewey Short Visitors Center to Lakeshore Trail to State Park Marina

(Asphalt surfaced trail)

Fast, out & back course; with a scenic view of Table Rock Lake every step of the way!

  • 5K will be one loop of 3.1 miles & Half Marathon is 3 loops of 4.36 miles each

Information: Race Director -Dennis James.  Please use the "Questions" button below.

To be sure you will receive the race t-shirt, you must register no later than May 25,2026.

We encourage participants to join us in our efforts to raise funds for the support of our mission.  See the fundraising tab on this website. We will present the runners whose fundraising income for the each of the races are the highest with a $25 store discount certificate at Fleet Feet Springfield.

Awards for each race: Trophies to Overall Male & Female; Overall Masters Male & Female (40 and above)

Age group awards (Male & Female) 14 & under, 15-19, 20-29, 30-39, 40-49, 50-59, 60+

All participants receive a finisher’s medal.

Awards for 5K will be at 8:30am, Half Marathon awards will be after the last runner comes across the finish line.

All race proceeds will go to the Local Branson BVA 913 Chapter. (All payments are final, no refunds are allowed.)
